title: Minimum Difference Between Largest and Smallest Value in Three Moves
hints: If the array has four or fewer elements, you can make them all equal in at most three moves, resulting in a difference of zero.
Sort the array first. Changing elements allows you to effectively ignore the smallest or largest values to minimize the range.
After sorting, consider removing k smallest and (3-k) largest elements for k from 0 to 3, then find the minimum difference between the new max and min.
